control-performance-trust.title: Control, Performance & Trust
control-performance-trust.subtitle: In Surveillance Society
control-performance-trust.summary: Through a theory-informed practice, I developed an interactive exhibit shaped by continuous reflection on surveillance studies alongside iterative prototyping. On a theoretical level, I tested the thesis that surveillance acts as a form of preemptive control, altering notions of trust and the performance of the self in interpersonal relationships. This research materialized in a responsive live-loop installation: a mirror that not only reflects the viewer but also tracks and visualizes their presence in real time. It overlays traces of previous observers, transforming the act of looking into both self-observation and the monitoring of others: Placing participants in the role of watcher and watched simultaneously.
control-performance-trust.outcome: The outcome is a responsive live-loop mirror installation that reflects viewers while tracking and visualizing their presence in real time.
control-performance-trust.tools:
TouchDesigner
Python
Camera tracking
Installation prototyping
control-performance-trust.supervisingProfessor:
Fourth Semester
Supervised by: Prof. Dr. Lasse Scherffig

quantum-abacus.title: Quantum Abacus
quantum-abacus.summary: Quantum Abacus is a kinetic installation that translates superposition and probabilistic measurement into a physical, abacus-inspired exhibit. It was developed in a competition for the Institute of AI Saftey of the German Aerospace Center (DLR) in Collaboration with Parsons School of Design in New York. The project demonstrates a novel approach to science communication by allowing visitors to interact with quantum concepts directly. Instead of explaining quantum principles through abstraction alone, the project lets visitors manipulate probabilities and directly experience in real time how a calculation can be made, with the otherwise un-imaginable quantum mechanics. The project tested the boundaries of what is possible with typical science-communication approaches, as the principles of quantum computing are utterly incompatble with metaphors in the atomic scale world. As the winners of the competition, Cora Kindermann and me were employed by the DLR.
quantum-abacus.context: Science-communication exhibit concept translating superposition and measurement into a tangible interaction.
quantum-abacus.approach:
Science communication,
Iterative Prototyping,
Iterative User Testing
quantum-abacus.outcome: Concept and prototype developed with partners at Parsons and DLR.
quantum-abacus.skills:
Physical Computing
Laser Cutting,
3D Printing,
Remote Collaboration,
Interface Design
quantum-abacus.supervisingProfessor:
Third Semester
Supervised by: Prof. Dr. Lasse Scherffig & Prof. Nina Juric

un-identikit.title: Un-Identikit
un-identikit.subtitle: Clothing for Solidarity in a Post-Body World
un-identikit.summary: Un-Identikit is a speculative fashion prototype that treats clothing as both a medium of expression and an infrastructure of protection in a post-apocalyptic, hyper-surveilled, permanent-crisis future. The project explores how garments can resist fixed identity assignment, and create collective visual codes that communicate anonymous solidarity instead.
un-identikit.context: Speculative fashion project imagining collective self-protection in a post-body surveillance regime.
un-identikit.approach:
Critical/Speculative design,
Material Storytelling,
Cultural Commentary
un-identikit.outcome: Concept and prototype emphasizing solidarity through shared visual codes.
un-identikit.skills:
Narrative Design,
Textile Prototyping,
Cultural Analysis
un-identikit.supervisingProfessor:
Fourth Semester
Supervised by: Patricia Hepp

reverse-engineered.title: Reverse Engineered
reverse-engineered.summary: Reverse Engineered is a compact product-semantics study that reworks familiar domestic objects through deliberately inverted material choices. By preserving recognizable forms while shifting expected material logic, the project tests how strongly functional meaning is tied to material cues and where interpretation begins to break, creating friction between what an object looks like and what it seems meant to do.
reverse-engineered.context: Material-semantics study on how substance alters the perceived purpose of familiar objects.
reverse-engineered.approach: Rebuilt domestic object archetypes with inverted material-function pairings as physical models.
reverse-engineered.outcome: A set of physical models that question how strongly functional meaning depends on material language.
reverse-engineered.tools:
Rapid model making,
Laser cutting,
3D printing,
Silicon Molding,
CNC
reverse-engineered.supervisingProfessor:
Second Semester
Supervised by: Prof. Jenz Großhans

happy-new-music-visuals.title: Happy New Music Visuals
happy-new-music-visuals.summary: This two-week collaboration remixes a cinematic reference into a short live performance that synchronizes image, sound, and stage dynamics. The project focuses on timing, atmosphere, and spatial rhythm, developing a responsive visual language that can support live audio performance while remaining coherent as a standalone moving-image piece.
happy-new-music-visuals.context: Live-performance collaboration translating a film reference into a short audio-visual set.
happy-new-music-visuals.approach: Built real-time image-and-sound behaviors in TouchDesigner and tuned them for stage timing.
happy-new-music-visuals.outcome: Live audio-visual performance with a synchronized visual system and highlight reel.
happy-new-music-visuals.skills:
Stage Design
Live Performance
Visual Storytelling
happy-new-music-visuals.supervisingProfessor:
Third Semester
Supervised by: Prof. Nina Juric

happy-birthday-widget.title: Happy Birthday Widget
happy-birthday-widget.summary: Happy Birthday Widget is an independently developed and marketed iOS birthday-reminder app centered on customizable home-screen widgets and lightweight daily use. Beyond interface design and development, the project included product positioning, branding, a website and marketing assets. An end-to-end exercise across concept, build, and distribution.
happy-birthday-widget.context: Independent product project spanning concept, build, launch, and promotion.
happy-birthday-widget.approach:
Privacy-First,
Human-centered design,
Design-led holistic product development
happy-birthday-widget.outcome: Shipped iOS app with marketing assets, website, and campaign material.
happy-birthday-widget.result:
iOS App
Marketing Assets
Website
Social Ads
happy-birthday-widget.tools:
Swift
SwiftUI
CoreData
App Store toolchain
happy-birthday-widget.skills:
End-to-end product lifecycle
Project management
Marketing

zeichenmeister.title: ZeichenMeister
zeichenmeister.summary: ZeichenMeister is a case study for a German sign-language learning interface that combines guided lesson flows with machine-learning-based sign detection. The project explores how onboarding, feedback, and practice structure can make sign-learning more accessible while balancing pedagogical clarity, technical feasibility, and a mobile-first interaction model.
zeichenmeister.context: Concept study for accessible German sign-language learning through guided interaction.
zeichenmeister.approach:
Human-centered design,
Iterative User Testing,
Rapid Prototyping
zeichenmeister.outcome: iOS app prototype and documented concept for iterative testing.
zeichenmeister.skills:
Product definition
User Flows
Rapid Prototyping
zeichenmeister.supervisingProfessor:
First Semester
Supervised by: Richard Jungkunz
